mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: COvariance shows CO-direction: Positive when both variables move in the same direction, negative when they move in opposite directions.

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: Correlation is COvariance's COusin, but standardized (divided by the standard deviations) to always be between -1 and 1.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: Covariance measures the direction of the linear relationship between two random variables.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: Correlation is a normalized version of covariance that provides both the direction and strength (-1 to +1) of the linear relationship.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A covariance of zero indicates no linear relationship, but the variables could still have a non-linear relationship.
